channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The White House is starting a new media policy that restricts wire services' access to the president
item.description: The White House has put forward a new media policy that sharply curtails access to Donald Trump by the news agencies that serve media outlets around the world.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Border deployment vehicle accident kills two service members, third in critical condition
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two service members deployed to the U.S. Southern border were killed and a third is in serious condition after a vehicle accident near Santa Teresa, New Mexico, the military announced late Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's lawyer says no proof of MS-13 affiliation
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's attorney, Benjamin Osorio, joined "CUOMO" for a live exclusive interview to discuss his client's status.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Tuesday convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gambian ex-soldier convicted at US trial of torturing suspected backers of a failed 2006 coup
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former member of Gambia's military was convicted in federal court Tuesday of torturing five people accused of involvement in a failed coup against the West African country's longtime dictator nearly 20 years ago, capping a rare prosecution in the United States for torture committed a
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about Hollywood icon Gene Hackman's and his wife's deaths
item.description: Authorities in northern New Mexico released police body camera video and other public records Tuesday in the investigation into the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa. The two were found dead Feb. 26 in their Santa Fe home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hertz says some customers' personal info exposed in breach
item.description: While the company did not say how many may have been impacted, they did identify the type of information that may have been exposed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KREX 5

item.title: Here's a behind-the-scenes look of the 'Wicked' Broadway tour as it flies into Utah
item.description: As "Wicked" returns to 'defy gravity' for Utah audiences, ABC4 got a peek backstage to see how the show all comes together.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested outside UnitedHealthcare is now charged with firearms counts and threatening violence
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A man who was arrested outside the headquarters of UnitedHealthcare had a history of mental health issues and threatened to shoot up corporate facilities, according to charges filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect in shooting at Dallas high school is in custody, officials say
item.description: School district officials say a suspect in a shooting at a Dallas high school that wounded four students and drew a heavy police response to the campus has been taken into custody.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge rejects defense that Gaudreau brothers contributed to their deaths by cycling while impaired
item.description: SALEM, N.J. (AP) — The family of NHL hockey player Johnny Gaudreau and his brother Matthew listened in pain Tuesday as lawyers debated whether the men's own drinking contributed to their deaths when they were hit by an allegedly drunken and enraged driver as they cycled at night.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: The park's African elephants instinctively circle the younger elephants to protect them when the earthquake struck.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, NewsNation

item.title: A look at the universities with federal funding targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: Harvard University is the latest in a growing list of higher education institutions that had its federal funding targeted by the government in order to comply with the Trump administration's political agenda. The series of threats — and subsequent pauses in funding — to some of the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Karen Read jury selection comes to a close, revving up 2nd murder trial in death of John O'Keefe
item.description: All 12 jurors and six alternates were empaneled Tuesday, clearing the way for opening statements. The case had needed just two more jurors since Friday came to a close with 16 of 18 selected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rules against parents who wore wristbands to protest transgender student athlete
item.description: A federal judge ruled Monday that two fathers cannot wear pink wristbands with two X’s — the symbol for female chromosomes — drawn on them in opposition to a transgender high school athlete on one of their daughters’ rival soccer teams.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel: Liberals 'viciously attacking comedians' are a 'big part of why Trump' won
item.description: "I think a lot of the outrage is completely manufactured, and it’s like, a lot of these people who are angry aren’t really angry," Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother releases first public statement
item.description: "I am speaking out today to begin the process of reclaiming my life and to have my say in how my story is told," he said.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries: Court should hold Trump officials in contempt over wrongly deported Maryland man
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Tuesday that the Supreme Court should hold members of the Trump administration in contempt of court if they don’t move to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported last month to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man confronts neighbor accused of shooting protected bird, video shows
item.description: A heated confrontation between a Florida homeowner and his neighbor over the shooting of a protected bird has prompted outrage in the community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Cox declares 'Declutter Day' in Utah
item.description: The Utah Division of Consumer Protection and Gov. Spencer J. Cox declared April 18, 2025 as the official Declutter Day in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge drops contempt case against ICE agent over arrest outside Boston courthouse
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge has dismissed a contempt case against an agent for the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement whose arrest of a man during his trial sparked criticism among the law enforcement community in Boston.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Study finds more people are obtaining abortions but fewer are traveling to other states for it
item.description: Fewer people crossed state lines to obtain abortions in 2024 than a year earlier, a new survey has found.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's teases return of Snack Wraps
item.description: McDonald’s may be one step closer to reintroducing its discontinued Snack Wraps, teasing a cryptic return date on social media Tuesday. On X, the company posted “snack wraps 0x.14.2025,” revealing the date the item will return but not the month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, KRON4

item.title: Researchers identify US counties with worst drinking water violations
item.description: The U.S. counties with the most egregious water quality violations are concentrated in four states: West Virginia, Pennsylvania, North Carolina and Oklahoma, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan illegal immigrant tackled by federal agents at New Hampshire courthouse after attempt to flee
item.description: A Venezuelan man illegally in the U.S. was tackled at a New Hampshire courthouse in February after federal agents attempted to detain him quietly in the elevator with no luck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WGN 9

item.title: Lawmakers push to meet with Salvadoran president to plead case of mistakenly deported man
item.description: Lawmakers are pushing to meet with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele while he’s in Washington amid broader efforts to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in the Central American nation. Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) sent a letter to El Salvador’s ambassador to the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4

item.title: All-female Blue Origin spaceflight launches from Texas
item.description: Six women are scheduled Monday to take the first all-female flight into space in over half a century.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NBA's West spots set, with the Nuggets, Clippers and Timberwolves in and the Warriors play-in bound
item.description: The Los Angeles Clippers worked overtime to clinch a playoff spot — and send the Golden State Warriors to the NBA's play-in tournament.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A beloved pet tortoise is reunited with its family weeks after disappearing in a Mississippi tornado
item.description: KOKOMO, Miss. (AP) — Myrtle, a cherished pet tortoise, has been reunited with its family in Mississippi weeks after disappearing during a deadly tornado outbreak in March. “He’s been through a lot,” said Myrtle's grateful owner, Tiffany Emanuel.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
